description: Command-line interface for Zvec vector database - manage collections, documents, and similarity searches from the terminal

userStories.title: Initialize Zvec Storage
userStories.description: As a user, I want to initialize a zvec storage location so that I can start managing vector collections.
acceptanceCriteria: `zvec init` creates storage directory at `~/.zvec/collections/` by default
`zvec init --path ./custom-path` creates storage at specified location
Command creates `.zvecrc` config file with storage path
Idempotent: re-running init on existing storage is safe
userStories.notes: Foundation command - must be completed first

userStories.title: Global Help and Version
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see help text and version info so that I can learn how to use the CLI.
userStories.notes: Foundation - can be developed in parallel with US-001

userStories.title: Configuration Management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure default settings so that I don't repeat common flags.
acceptanceCriteria: `zvec config set storage.path ./custom-path` sets config value
`zvec config get storage.path` shows config value
`zvec config list` shows all config values
userStories.notes: Foundation - can be developed in parallel with US-001

userStories.title: Create Collection
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a new collection with a defined schema so that I can store documents with vectors.
acceptanceCriteria: Support multiple vector fields: `--vector embedding:1536 --vector thumbnail:512`
Default vector field name is "embedding" if not specified
Error message if collection already exists

userStories.title: List Collections
userStories.description: As a user, I want to list all collections so that I can see what's available.
acceptanceCriteria: `zvec collection list` (or `zvec ls`) shows all collections
Default output: table with columns (name, documents, vectors, created)
userStories.notes: Requires collection creation capability for testing

userStories.title: Inspect Collection Schema
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view a collection's schema and stats so that I understand its structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Output includes: field names, types, vector dimensions
Output includes collection stats (document count, size)
userStories.notes: Requires collection creation capability for testing

userStories.title: Drop Collection
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete a collection so that I can remove unused data.
acceptanceCriteria: Requires `--force` or `-y` flag to confirm (no interactive prompt in v1)
userStories.notes: Requires collection creation capability for testing

userStories.title: Insert Documents
userStories.description: As a user, I want to insert documents with vectors so that I can populate my collection.
acceptanceCriteria: `zvec insert <collection> --file data.json` inserts from JSON file
Support CSV with vector columns: `--file data.csv --vector-col "embedding"`
Single document via CLI: `zvec insert <collection> --id doc1 --vector "0.1,0.2,0.3" --field title="Hello"`
Batch insert with progress indicator for large files
userStories.notes: Complex command with multiple input formats - largest story

userStories.title: Fetch Document by ID
userStories.description: As a user, I want to retrieve a document by ID so that I can inspect its contents.
acceptanceCriteria: Default output: formatted table of fields
`--vector` flag to include vector data (hidden by default)
Error message if document not found
userStories.notes: Requires documents to exist for meaningful testing

userStories.title: Delete Documents
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete documents so that I can remove unwanted data.
acceptanceCriteria: `--dry-run` flag shows what would be deleted without deleting
userStories.notes: Requires documents to exist for meaningful testing

userStories.title: Similarity Search
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search for similar vectors so that I can find related documents.
acceptanceCriteria: `--vector-file query.json` loads query vector from file
Default output: table with columns (id, score, [selected fields])
userStories.notes: Core feature - requires documents with vectors for testing

userStories.title: Optimize Collection
userStories.description: As a user, I want to optimize a collection so that queries are faster.
userStories.notes: Requires documents in collection for meaningful optimization
